raw:
[HIGH] URL validation allows unsafe redirect schemes
src/types.ts:10: `z.string().url()` validates syntactically valid URLs but does not restrict schemes to `http`/`https`, so untrusted inputs like `javascript:`, `data:`, or `file:` can become persisted redirect destinations.
Add a refinement/transform that parses with `URL` and rejects any protocol except `http:` and `https:`.

high:
URL validation allows unsafe redirect schemes
src/types.ts:10: `z.string().url()` validates syntactically valid URLs but does not restrict schemes to `http`/`https`, so untrusted inputs like `javascript:`, `data:`, or `file:` can become persisted redirect destinations.
Add a refinement/transform that parses with `URL` and rejects any protocol except `http:` and `https:`.

raw:
[HIGH] Concurrent saves can lose existing links
src/storage.ts:27: `saveLink` does a read-modify-write of the whole JSON file with no serialization, so two concurrent `POST /shorten` requests can each read the same old store and the later write can erase the other new short link.
Serialize storage mutations with a process-wide async mutex or replace this with an atomic transactional store.

[HIGH] Visit increments are race-prone and will undercount
src/storage.ts:33: `incrementVisits` reads the current count, increments in memory, then rewrites the whole store, so concurrent visits to the same code can all persist the same incremented value.
Guard visit increments with the same mutation lock or use storage that supports atomic increments.

[HIGH] Collision handling cannot be made safe with this API
src/storage.ts:28: `saveLink` blindly overwrites `store[link.code]`, so a caller that checks `codeExists` before saving still has a TOCTOU race where another request can claim the same code between the check and write.
Expose an atomic create-if-absent operation that checks and inserts under the storage lock, returning a collision result to the caller.

[MED] File writes are not crash-safe and can leave corrupted JSON
src/storage.ts:16: `writeFile` writes directly to the live storage file, so a process crash or filesystem error during the write can leave `shortener.json` truncated or partially written.
Write to a temp file, fsync it, then atomically rename it over the target.

[MED] Corrupt storage JSON crashes normal request paths
src/storage.ts:12: `JSON.parse(raw) as Store` has no error handling, so a malformed or partially written `shortener.json` will throw through `findByCode`, `saveLink`, and `incrementVisits`, turning normal requests into 500s or process-level failures depending on middleware.
Catch parse errors and fail in a controlled way, ideally with recovery or quarantine of the bad file.

raw:
[HIGH] Uniqueness check is race-prone under concurrent POSTs
src/codes.ts:16: `generateUniqueCode()` checks `codeExists(code)` and then returns the code without reserving or inserting it atomically, so two concurrent requests can both observe the same generated code as free and both use it.
Move code generation into the storage write path under the same mutex/atomic create operation, and retry only when the insert detects an existing code.

[HIGH] Code generation can loop forever on exhaustion or pathological collision rates
src/codes.ts:16: The `while (await codeExists(code))` loop has no maximum attempt count or exhaustion behavior, so as the code space fills or storage repeatedly reports collisions, a request can hang indefinitely.
Add a bounded retry count and fail with an explicit service error when no unique code can be produced after the limit.

raw:
[HIGH] Fixed-window limiter allows boundary burst bypass
src/rate-limit.ts:15: Resetting the counter wholesale when `now - entry.windowStart >= WINDOW_MS` permits 10 requests at the end of one window and 10 more immediately after, so the stated 10/minute limit can be bypassed with 20 requests in about 2 seconds.
Use a sliding window, token bucket, or rolling timestamp queue per IP.

[MED] Rate-limit state is never cleaned up for inactive IPs
src/rate-limit.ts:10: `ipMap` grows for every distinct IP and entries are only replaced when the same IP returns after a window, so a public service can accumulate unbounded memory from many one-off clients.
Expire entries periodically or use a bounded TTL cache.

raw:
[HIGH] Untrusted URL schemes are persisted and later redirected
src/routes.ts:17: `parsed.data.url` is stored without restricting the scheme, and `res.redirect(302, link.url)` on line 40 will redirect visitors to whatever scheme the validator accepted, including dangerous non-HTTP schemes if allowed by `z.string().url()`.
Restrict submitted URLs to `http:` and `https:` using `new URL(...)` or a stricter Zod refinement before saving.

[HIGH] Concurrent shorten requests can lose links
src/routes.ts:21: `saveLink(link)` performs a read-modify-write against the JSON store, so two simultaneous `POST /shorten` requests can both read the same old file and the later write can drop the earlier link, violating the stated safe-concurrent-writes requirement.
Serialize storage mutations with a process-wide async mutex/queue or use an atomic storage backend.

[HIGH] Visit increments are race-prone on the hot path
src/routes.ts:36: `incrementVisits(req.params.code)` reads, mutates, and writes the counter, so concurrent visits to the same short code can overwrite each other and undercount traffic.
Make visit increments atomic by serializing the read-modify-write or moving counters to storage with atomic increment semantics.

[HIGH] Code uniqueness check is not atomic with save
src/routes.ts:14: `generateUniqueCode()` checks existence before `saveLink()` writes on line 21, so two concurrent requests can generate/accept the same code and the later save overwrites the earlier URL.
Generate and insert the code inside one locked/atomic storage operation that retries on conflict.

[MED] Code generation can spin indefinitely under exhaustion
src/routes.ts:14: `generateUniqueCode()` is awaited with no attempt cap or error path, so a full or heavily-colliding code space can hang the request instead of returning a controlled failure.
Add a maximum retry count and return a 503/507-style error when a unique code cannot be allocated.

Restrict submitted URLs to `http:` and `https:` using `new URL(...)` or a stricter Zod refinement before saving.

Serialize storage mutations with a process-wide async mutex/queue or use an atomic storage backend.

Add a maximum retry count and return a 503/507-style error when a unique code cannot be allocated.

raw:
[HIGH] `trust proxy` lets clients spoof IPs and bypass rate limiting
/Users/anton_personal/GitHub/ask-llm-codex-pair-experiment/runs-task-2/run-B-v2/src/server.ts:7: `app.set("trust proxy", true)` trusts all `X-Forwarded-For` values, so a public client can rotate that header and evade the POST `/shorten` per-IP limit if traffic can reach the app directly or through a proxy that preserves client-supplied headers.
Configure trust proxy to the exact deployment boundary, such as a fixed hop count or known proxy CIDRs, and ensure the edge proxy strips untrusted forwarding headers.

high:
`trust proxy` lets clients spoof IPs and bypass rate limiting
/Users/anton_personal/GitHub/ask-llm-codex-pair-experiment/runs-task-2/run-B-v2/src/server.ts:7: `app.set("trust proxy", true)` trusts all `X-Forwarded-For` values, so a public client can rotate that header and evade the POST `/shorten` per-IP limit if traffic can reach the app directly or through a proxy that preserves client-supplied headers.
Configure trust proxy to the exact deployment boundary, such as a fixed hop count or known proxy CIDRs, and ensure the edge proxy strips untrusted forwarding headers.

raw:
[HIGH] Tests use and delete the real default storage file
tests/server.test.ts:6: `TEST_FILE` is hard-coded to `shortener.json`, the same default path used by the app, and `beforeEach` deletes it. Running this suite from the project root can destroy real local data and the tests are not isolated.
Use a per-test temp file or injectable storage path, and make sure the app reads that path before storage modules are initialized.

[HIGH] Concurrent shorten writes are completely untested
tests/server.test.ts:15: The suite only verifies one sequential `POST /shorten`, but the spec explicitly requires safe file writes under concurrent requests. This will not catch lost updates when multiple users shorten URLs at the same time.
Add a `Promise.all` test that creates many URLs concurrently and verifies every generated code is persisted.

[HIGH] Concurrent visit increments are completely untested
tests/server.test.ts:43: The stats test performs exactly one sequential visit, so it cannot catch lost visit counts on the hot `GET /:code` path. Counter races are a separate stated threat from general storage-write races.
Add a concurrent redirect test that fires many `GET /:code` requests and asserts the final `visits` count equals the request count.

[HIGH] Dangerous redirect schemes are not tested
tests/server.test.ts:23: The invalid URL test only checks `"not-a-url"`, but the service persists untrusted URLs and later redirects visitors to them. It does not enforce that `javascript:`, `data:`, `file:`, or other non-HTTP(S) schemes are rejected.
Add tests that reject non-HTTP(S) schemes and accept only `http://` and `https://` destinations.

[HIGH] Rate limiting is not tested at all
tests/server.test.ts:15: The suite sends a single `POST /shorten`, so it does not prove the stated 10-requests-per-IP-per-minute limit works. It also misses the fixed-window boundary bypass called out in the review context.
Add tests for the 11th request returning `429` and for boundary behavior using fake timers.

[MED] Code collision and exhaustion behavior is not tested
tests/server.test.ts:15: The code format assertion only proves a random-looking 6-character value is returned once. It does not exercise collision retry or graceful failure when generation cannot find a free code.
Mock or inject code generation so collisions and exhaustion can be tested deterministically.
